title: NGE vs Rebuilds soundtrack
wikitext:
[]: NGE vs. Rebuild's soundtrack [No Spoilers]             Quote Post by terminus » Mon Jun 02, 2008 11:21 pm Do you think Rebuild's soundtrack will have the potential to be even better than the previous Evangelion episodes and movie soundtracks?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Jun 02, 2008 11:48 pm Don't know. Concerning the old tracks a lot of peoples preference is fully dependent on personal preferences. The new tracks I love and hopefully this means that each Angel battle will have its own sound. But damn it, I'm waiting for anything with Eva 2.0. That's bound to be a good soundtrack.

[]:             Quote Post by Master Hand » Tue Jun 03, 2008 12:28 am I hope the BEAST 2 is remixed.The first one was sweet.I bet the second one would sound awsome too.

[]:             Quote Post by iamsynch » Tue Jun 03, 2008 1:27 am I thought it's going pretty well so far. I think Rebuild's soundtrack is an improvement over the music from the 1st 6 episodes. Compare the music when Shinji meets his dad again; the one used in 1.0 is a lot better I think.

[]:             Quote Post by Master Hand » Tue Jun 03, 2008 2:13 am Yeah.I think some of rebuilds music is better then the original as well.

[]: Re: Will Rebuild's soundtrack top the previous Eva movies an             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Jun 03, 2008 4:45 am terminus wrote: Do you think Rebuild's soundtrack will have the potential to be even better than the previous Evangelion episodes and movie soundtracks?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 03, 2008 2:21 pm I think Sagisu's music with Rebuild will at least be equal to that of EoE/stuff from the actual series if nothing else. Not that I'm complaing if it WOULD be better than we what already have, because I love Sagisu's stuff.

[]: Re: Will Rebuild's soundtrack top the original series?             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Jun 03, 2008 3:11 pm terminus wrote: Will Rebuild's soundtrack top the original series?

[]: Re: Will Rebuild's soundtrack top the original series?             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Jun 03, 2008 3:26 pm NAveryW wrote: terminus wrote: Will Rebuild's soundtrack top the original series?

[]: Re: Will Rebuild's soundtrack top the original series?             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Tue Jun 03, 2008 3:27 pm Xard wrote: ...huh, it has been exactly same apart from few songs such as horrible beautiful world and average Angel of Doom

[]: Re: Will Rebuild's soundtrack top the original series?             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Jun 03, 2008 3:30 pm IrkenEvangelion wrote: Xard wrote: ...huh, it has been exactly same apart from few songs such as horrible beautiful world and average Angel of Doom

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Tue Jun 03, 2008 3:32 pm Yea, I agree it's your typical pop song, but, I don't know, I think it sounds cool

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Jun 03, 2008 6:50 pm The drums in Decisive Battle sound a bit tinny to me.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Jun 03, 2008 7:14 pm It has certainly not been exactly the same! Compare the tracks side by side, and the new versions are usually definitely played by a better orchestra. I will admit, though, that Decisive Battle is almost exactly the same with a few more drums.

[]: Re: Will Rebuild's soundtrack top the original series?             Quote Post by terminus » Tue Jun 03, 2008 9:21 pm Xard wrote: NAveryW wrote: terminus wrote: Will Rebuild's soundtrack top the original series?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Jun 03, 2008 9:25 pm KST was a completely different style of music. You can't really compare them. Furthermore, KST's music was very derivative of works such as A Whiter Shade of Pale and Hey, Jude . EDIT: After listening to it again, though, I will agree that it is still a very powerful song. DOUBLE EDIT: However, I believe most of its power comes from the context, and it wouldn't be nearly as moving to someone who isn't familiar with NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by iamsynch » Tue Jun 03, 2008 11:52 pm NAveryW wrote: KST was a completely different style of music. You can't really compare them. Furthermore, KST's music was very derivative of works such as A Whiter Shade of Pale and Hey, Jude . EDIT: After listening to it again, though, I will agree that it is still a very powerful song. DOUBLE EDIT: However, I believe most of its power comes from the context, and it wouldn't be nearly as moving to someone who isn't familiar with NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jun 04, 2008 12:37 am iamsynch wrote: What about Expansion of Blockade? Nothing from the Rebuild OST comes close.

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Wed Jun 04, 2008 10:50 am NAveryW wrote: iamsynch wrote: What about Expansion of Blockade? Nothing from the Rebuild OST comes close.

[]:             Quote Post by C4 » Fri Jun 06, 2008 1:41 am It's a little bit unfair to compare 1.0's soundtrack to EoE's: the material of the latter is far more interesting, as the whole movie is obviously the payday-- erm, excuse, payoff-- to the series. The events are far more dramatic, and demand more stunning music. 1.0 is nothing more than an introduction, so it doesn't require the dramatic forces that will be necessary later. It would be more fitting to compare it to the series' score up until this point; and everything is largely the same this time around. The battle tracks are given a whole lot more percussion, there are still about four different versions of Rei's theme that are essentially the same, and new orchestrations of the "Hedgehog's Dilemma" theme. Strangely enough, though that was one of my favorite themes from the series, I feel as if it were overused in 1.0. The two new additions should be an indicator of the direction the music is going in. Sagisu's music, throughout all his different series, has always had a period, 70's feel, which is heavy sometimes, and light others. I don't get that same kind of feeling from these new tracks, and I love the both of them, I think they just sound more professional. "I'll Go On Loving Someone Else" is my favorite, partially because it reminds me of some of his more brooding piano work on the Kare Kano OSTs, and I think it fits Shinji well. It drags its ass at under a beat per second, and you wish it would hurry up and get its act together, but it just wants to stay depressing, and you can't help but feel sort of bad, which also describes my feelings towards Shinji. As the movies keep coming out, I'd imagine that they will feature less music from the series, and provide us with many new themes that I am confident will rival EoE's soundtrack.(Which, in my opinion, is the best soundtrack I have listened to, and as a former film scoring student, I have listened to a lot.)
summary:
[]: NGE vs. Rebuild's soundtrack [No Spoilers]             Quote Post by terminus » Mon Jun 02, 2008 11:21 pm Do you think Rebuild's soundtrack will have the potential to be even better than the previous Evangelion episodes and movie soundtracks?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Jun 02, 2008 11:48 pm Don't know. Concerning the old tracks a lot of peoples prefere

Article: NGE vs Rebuilds soundtrack
 events are far more dramatic, and demand more stunning music. 1.0 is nothing more than an introduction, so it doesn't require the dramatic forces that will be necessary later. It would be more fitting to compare it to the series' score up until this point; and everything is largely the same this time around. The battle tracks are given a whole lot more percussion, there are still about four different versions of Rei's theme that are essentially the same, and new orchestrations of the "Hedgehog's Dilemma" theme. Strangely enough, though that was one of my favorite themes from the series, I feel as if it were overused in 1.0. The two new additions should be an indicator of the direction the music is going in. Sagisu's music, throughout all his different series, has always had a period, 70's feel, which is heavy sometimes, and light others. I don't get that same kind of feeling from these new tracks, and I love the both of them, I think they just sound more professional. "I'll Go On Loving Someone Else" is my favorite, partially because it reminds me of some of his more brooding piano work on the Kare Kano OSTs, and I think it fits Shinji well. It drags its ass at under a beat per second, and you wish it would hurry up and get its act together, but it just wants to stay depressing, and you can't help but feel sort of bad, which also describes my feelings towards Shinji. As the movies keep coming out, I'd imagine that they will feature less music from the series, and provide us with many new themes that I am confident will rival EoE's soundtrack.(Which, in my opinion, is the best soundtrack I have listened to, and as a former film scoring student, I have listened to a lot.)
